SHOULD NOT: SHOULD NOT check to see whether the Layer 2 address used was multicast or not

Mandatory: are stored serially in the "options" field
with no padding between the options

MUST: MUST be chosen to be unique among the IAIDs for that IA type on the client
MUST be consistent across restarts of the DHCP client

MUST NOT: the data areas of the options MUST NOT be concatenated or otherwise combined

Mandatory: the client must retransmit its message
The client MUST update an "elapsed-time" value
a client MUST listen for a Reconfigure
Recommended: The algorithm SHOULD produce a different sequence of random numbers

Mandatory: All DHCP messages sent between clients and servers share an identical fixed-format header and a variable-format area for options
All values in the message header and in options are in network byte order
Options are stored serially in the "options" field, with no padding between the options
Options are byte-aligned but are not aligned in any other way (such as on 2-byte or 4-byte boundaries)
